name: non-finite-rejection
description: Canonicalization MUST reject non-finite numbers per attestation.md Section 4.1

tests.name: nan_lowercase
tests.description: NaN is not valid JSON and must be rejected

tests.name: infinity_positive
tests.description: Infinity is not valid JSON and must be rejected

tests.name: infinity_negative
tests.description: -Infinity is not valid JSON and must be rejected

parsing_notes.json_standard: RFC 8259 does not permit NaN, Infinity, or -Infinity as JSON number values
parsing_notes.parser_behavior: Conforming JSON parsers will reject these as syntax errors during parsing
parsing_notes.defense_in_depth: Implementations should also check for non-finite values after float parsing in case of lenient parsers

implementation_check.go: json.Decoder rejects NaN/Infinity as invalid JSON syntax
implementation_check.javascript: JSON.parse() rejects NaN/Infinity as invalid JSON syntax

notes: Per attestation.md Section 4.1: Canonicalization MUST reject non-finite numbers. NaN, Infinity, and -Infinity MUST be treated as invalid JSON values. Standard JSON parsers already reject these, but implementations should verify this behavior.
